title: Success Stories: Driving the Future of Clean Energy: EB2-NIW Approved for Solar Materials Scientist Advancing Perovskite Technologies
content:
Client’s Testimonial:
“I want to express my sincere gratitude to the entire team for their exceptional work throughout my petition process. From the beginning, the professionalism, expertise, and dedication displayed by everyone involved were outstanding. The attention to detail in preparing and documenting my case was remarkable, and I was consistently impressed by how seamlessly every step was handled. The clarity and efficiency with which the process was navigated made a challenging experience much easier, and I am deeply appreciative of the hard work and commitment shown. Communication was another standout feature of my experience. The timely responses and thorough guidance I received instilled confidence and support every step of the way. I extend my best wishes for continued success and prosperity for the firm. It has truly been a pleasure working with such an invaluable team, and I look forward to future opportunities to collaborate. Thank you once again for your exceptional support.”
On May 22 nd , 2025, we received another EB-2 NIW ( National Interest Waiver ) approval for a Research Scientist in the Field of Sustainable Chemical Science ( Approval Notice ).
General Field: Sustainable Chemical Science
Position at the Time of Case Filing: Research Scientist
Country of Origin: Ethiopia
State of Residence at the Time of Filing: Vermont
Approval Notice Date: May 22 nd , 2025
Processing Time: 4 months, 16 days (Premium Processing Requested)
We are proud to share the EB2- NIW ( National Interest Waiver ) approval of a remarkable materials scientist from Ethiopia whose pioneering research in perovskite solar cells and sustainable photocatalytic materials is accelerating the U.S. transition to clean energy. With expertise in engineering durable and efficient solar devices, this scientist is helping overcome key barriers in commercial solar technology.
Pioneering Renewable Solutions Through Chemical Innovation
The client’s proposed endeavor focuses on developing low-cost, scalable, and efficient large-area perovskite solar cells (PSCs) using novel thin-film coating, precursor, and interface engineering techniques. By integrating eco-friendly fabrication methods and next-generation nanomaterials, he aims to enhance the efficiency, longevity, and manufacturability of solar modules, critical to making solar energy more accessible and reliable.
In tandem, he is also researching photocatalytic materials for industrial wastewater remediation, addressing both energy generation and environmental sustainability in one synergistic research framework.
A Record of High-Impact Research and Global Recognition
The client has published 18 peer-reviewed journal articles, with 13 first- or co-first-authored, and has received a total of 379 citations. His publications have appeared in elite journals such as Advanced Materials, Chemical Engineering Journal, ACS Applied Materials & Interfaces, and Journal of Environmental Chemical Engineering.
Thirteen of his papers rank among the top 1%, 10%, or 20% most-cited chemistry articles for their respective publication years, demonstrating exceptional influence in solar cell engineering, thin-film materials, and photocatalysis.
In recognition of his impact, he has been invited to peer-review for top journals like CrystEngComm, Langmuir, and New Journal of Chemistry, having completed at least six formal reviews to date.
Federal Support and Broad Scientific Endorsement
His research has garnered funding from the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) and the National Science Foundation (NSF) —a testament to its alignment with national goals in sustainable energy and technology innovation.
A leading academic and department chair wrote:
“[Client’s] work brings us closer to commercial viability in solar energy and wastewater remediation. His innovative use of perovskite structures and nanomaterial integration boosts both efficiency and environmental performance, with broad implications for U.S. energy independence and industrial sustainability.”
Technical Contributions of National Interest
Among his landmark contributions:
● Enhanced PSC efficiency using tetraoctylammonium chloride passivation, silver nanoparticle doping, and surface interface engineering—boosting both charge extraction and light absorption.
● Eco-friendly slot-die coating techniques for scalable solar film production.
● Innovative photocatalysts, such as AgLaTi2O6, have been shown to degrade industrial dyes under visible light with high reusability.
● These innovations serve dual national goals: meeting federal clean energy targets and advancing sustainable industrial practices in water treatment and chemical waste management.
Filed under premium processing on January 6, 2025, and approved by May 22, 2025, this EB2- NIW petition was strongly supported by clear evidence of national impact, scholarly excellence, and technological leadership.
We proudly celebrate the achievements of global researchers whose work supports America’s innovation economy and environmental leadership. This client’s success story highlights how clean energy and clean water can be pursued together, powered by science, and made real by visionaries like him.
--------------------------------------------
North America Immigration Law Group (Chen Immigration Law Associates) is a U.S. immigration law firm dedicated to representing corporations, research institutions, and individuals from all 50 U.S. states regarding I-140 immigration petitions. We specialize in employment-based immigration petition and have a proven record of high success rate for the categories of: EB2- NIW ( National Interest Waiver ), EB1-A ( Alien of Extraordinary Ability ), EB1-B (Outstanding Researcher/Professor) and O-1 ( Alien of Extraordinary Ability ).
Our Sixty-four Thousand I-140 Approvals Provide Unprecedented Insight into the USCIS Adjudication Trend
With nearly 64,000 EB-1A , EB-1B , EB-2 NIW and O-1 cases approved, we have first hand information on the manner in which the USCIS adjudicate I-140 cases. As the USCIS has constantly changed its adjudication standards for the EB-1A , EB-1B and EB-2 NIW categories, our firm's huge database of successful cases gives you unprecedented insight to USCIS adjudication trends. We carefully analyze the data for all of our cases and apply the results of our analyses toward giving our clients up-to-date advice and adapting our strategies such that we remain on par with the ever-shifting landscape of immigration law in the U.S. With us, you will always have access to important updates, strategies, and information so that you can make the most informed decisions about your case.
We Have Helped Tens of Thousands of Clients with Credentials and Backgrounds Similar to Yours
With our exceedingly large number of successful petitions, no matter what credentials you have, no matter your background and field of expertise, no matter your visa status or nationality, chances are we have helped hundreds or even thousands of clients just like you. Our clients are usually impressed with how well we understand their research and work. Our insight and understanding stems from the fact that we have handled many cases with elements similar to yours already, and this helps us devise the best strategies for each individual petition.
Vast Majority of Clients Came to Us Because of Referrals
For years, our firm has attracted new clients based solely on word of mouth, recommendations, and the positive collaboration experiences shared with them by their friends and family. We take pride in our reputation and work hard to ensure that we provide a green card application experience that our clients are happy to share with their friends and colleagues. That is how our cumulative total of approved cases grew from 600 in 2013 to nearly 64,000 in 2025.
